input.task: Identify domain contamination in engine files
input.context: AIOS engine (scripts/) contains 1402 files. AP-001 rule prohibits domain-specific terms in engine layer.

reasoning: Scanned all engine files (excluding clients/) for domain-specific terms using case-insensitive regex. Found 43 contamination instances across 18 files. Classified into 4 risk tiers based on contamination density and file purpose: HIGH (scripts that are 100% client-specific — squad generators), MEDIUM-Orange (WhatsApp pipeline scripts), MEDIUM-Yellow (engine core with leaked terms in detection lists), LOW (documentation with examples). Key insight: self-correction.js and ia-council-engine.js contain domain terms INTENTIONALLY — they are contamination DETECTORS, not contaminated code. These are semi-false-positives requiring externalization, not removal.

output.falsePositiveNotes: self-correction.js + ia-council-engine.js use terms for detection (AP-001 check)
